mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, the Fed maintains a restrictive monetary stance that keeps long-term Treasury yields elevated. Sustained FEDFUNDS above this threshold signals continued yield pressure, driving 10-year Treasury yields upward over the next 20 trading days as markets price in persistent rate expectations.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and economic growth expectations. Accelerating RSAFS growth over 3 months triggers inflation expectations and Fed tightening premia, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher within the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in more than 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between RSAFS growth and 20-day yield change drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate below 4.5% indicates tight labor markets and wage pressure risk. When UNRATE falls below this threshold, markets anticipate inflation acceleration and Fed policy persistence, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE < 4.5%, or Spearman rank correlation < 0.08 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 102.0 combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 332.0) signals supply-constrained growth. This interaction triggers stagflation expectations, forcing the Fed to maintain higher rates and pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term's marginal contribution to yield direction prediction is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 1400 thousand units signal weakening construction demand and future economic drag. Sustained low HOUST triggers growth recession expectations, causing investors to flee to safety and driving 10-year Treasury yields downward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 55% of instances when HOUST < 1400, or Rank IC > -0.05 (fails to show negative correlation).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating below 100 thousand per month combined with rising unemployment signals labor market deterioration. This interaction triggers recession risk premia, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall sharply over the next 20 trading days as safe-haven demand increases.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 100 AND UNRATE rising > 0.2pp month-over-month
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or interaction term explains less than 5% of incremental variance in yield direction.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) sustained above 332.0 forces the Fed to maintain restrictive policy. Over a 60-day horizon, persistent high inflation erodes real yields and anchors nominal 10-year Treasury yields at elevated levels, preventing downward movement despite potential growth weakness.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 50 basis points over 60-day periods when CPIAUCSL > 332.0 in more than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2.0% annualized combined with falling retail sales (RSAFS declining month-over-month) signals recession onset. This interaction triggers flight-to-quality flows, driving 10-year Treasury yields downward over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations collapse.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 2.0% annualized AND RSAFS month-over-month change < 0%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 45% of co-triggered instances over 60-day windows, or the interaction's predictive power decays below Rank IC of -0.08 in any rolling 2-year period.
